samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.

The method of empty and full judgment is to generate empty and full signals by comparing the Gray code. Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth 8. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower three digits of the binary number as the address to access RAM. When the read and write Pointers are equal, the FIFO is null.

When the write pointer has one more cycle RAM than the read pointer, the highest and second-highest bits of the read and write pointer are opposite, the remaining bits are the same, and the FIFO is full. Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations. Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations. Active-low reset signal for both read and write operations. Write increment signal. Triggered when a new data is written. Read increment signal. Triggered when data is read. Write data input. Data to be written into the FIFO. Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations. Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations. Read data output. Data read from the FIFO. The bit width of FIFO, which can be configured. The depth of FIFO, which can be configured.

1. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule:

<module id="dual_port_RAM"> <description>Implement a dual-port RAM module.&description> <parameter> <param id="DEPTH" value="16"/> <param id="WIDTH" value="8"/> </parameter> <ports> <input id="wclk"/> <input id="wenc"/> <input id="waddr" width_description="$clog2(DEPTH)"/> <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]"/> <input id="rclk"/> <input id="renc"/> <input id="raddr" width_description="$clog2(DEPTH)"/> <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]"/> </ports> <logic_description> <item>The module implements a dual-port RAM using a two-dimensional array, RAM_MEM, with a size of DEPTH by WIDTH.&item> <item>The module supports simultaneous read and write operations.&item> <item>The module uses a write clock (wclk) for write operations.&item> <item>The module uses a read clock (rclk) for read operations.&item> <item>The module uses a write enable signal (wenc) to control write operations.&item> <item>The module uses a read enable signal (renc) to control read operations.&item> <item>The module writes data to RAM_MEM at the address specified by waddr when wenc is high.&item> <item>The module reads data from RAM_MEM at the address specified by raddr when renc is high.&item> </logic_description> <implementation>Uses a two-dimensional array to store data in RAM_MEM.&implementation> </module>

2. Implement write and read pointer controllers:

<module id="asyn_fifo"> <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO with FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.</description> <ports> <input id="wclk"/> <input id="rclk"/> <input id="rstn"/> <input id="winc"/> <input id="rinc"/> <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]"/> <output id="wfull"/> <output id="rempty"/> <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]"/> </ports> <parameter> <param id="WIDTH" value="8"/> <param id="DEPTH" value="16"/> </parameter> <logic_description> <item>The module implements an asynchronous FIFO using a dual-port RAM and two controllers for write and read operations.&item> <item>The module supports asynchronous operation, allowing independent clock signals for write and read operations.&item> <item>The module includes a reset signal for both write and read operations.&item> <item>The module includes separate controllers for managing write and read operations.&item> <item>The module synchronizes the read and write pointers using Gray code to avoid glitches.&item> </logic_description> <implementation>Uses dual-port RAM to store data and manages write and read operations with separate controllers.&implementation> </module>

3. Implement the read pointer synchronizer using the two-stage trigger of the write clock:

<module id="asyn_fifo"> <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO with FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect
